name: og-tags are table-stakes for exhibit publishers
description: Survey for GOAL.md §5 (2026-06-09): Omeka S (SEO module + theme og: tags), Scalar (og: + Dublin Core per page), Exhibit.so / StoryMapJS (og:title/description/image unfurls), WordPress GLAM stacks (Yoast). Audience: GLAM creators, educators, independent researchers sharing published exhibit links via social/chat/email — the unfurl card is the exhibit's first impression; a bare URL reads untrustworthy. og:title/og:description/og:type/twitter:card work without a canonical origin; og:image needs an ABSOLUTE url (deploy origin / PUBLISH_BASE) — defer that part.

name: IIIF Manifest pull + CSV import — highest-ROI contributor funnel-wideners
description: cozy-iiif parseURL+P2→P3 (PURE, no Node) enables paste-a-manifest-URL bootstrap for GLAM/DH audiences. CSV→annotation bulk import (papaparse shape) converts spreadsheet-native non-technical contributors. Both are pure browser, low effort, no server. Source: Prior Art/14-import-interop.md cozy-iiif section; field-studio exceljs shape.

name: Async-zip DAG = correct serverless-native collaboration primitive
description: Yjs binary encoding (Y.encodeStateAsUpdate) validates: compact diff transport = zip payload. Live co-editing (liiive/Hocuspocus) requires WebRTC signaling server — server-shaped. Best serverless approximation: async-zip merge UI with named collaborative pass + Readings as isolated interpretive passes. Source: Prior Art/10-state-mutation-patterns.md liiive section; y-webrtc README.

name: In-browser ONNX AI (FastSAM) feasible as lazy-loaded Worker — model as static GH-Pages asset
description: browser-visual-search PURE engine: ort.InferenceSession.create(url,{executionProviders:['wasm']}) lazy-memoized. FastSAM bbox→FragmentSelector xywh=pixel: clean WADM mapping (~20-40 MB lazy). CLIP-ViT-B/32 (~340 MB) too heavy. Whisper ASR + BLIP captioning are server-shaped. Source: Prior Art/19-ai-authoring.md browser-visual-search/src/segmentation/segment.ts:8-11.

name: Build-time SEO + IIIF Change Discovery static pages = zero-cost discoverability
description: IIIF Change Discovery API 1.0 OrderedCollection is static JSON harvestable by OCLC/Europeana/DPLA aggregators when generated at publish time. og:tags + Schema.org CreativeWork JSON-LD + Astro sitemap = pure build step. Static oEmbed JSON per exhibit covers 80% social unfurl without a dynamic endpoint. Source: IIIF Discovery API 1.0 spec (iiif.io); oEmbed spec §2.4.

name: GitHub OAuth PKCE = serverless identity bridge: local display name → citable public author
description: Decap-CMS PKCE S256 pattern (crypto.subtle, sessionStorage verifier, popup postMessage origin check) is reference for browser-only OAuth from SPA. Same credential for GH-Pages publish doubles as contributor attribution (GitHub username as citable author). DID signing is Phase 2. Source: Prior Art/20-security-sanitization.md decap-cms section; CONTEXT.md §95.

name: OffscreenCanvas DZI Worker tiling (v1.1) — liftable seed from field-studio, wasm-vips unsafe
description: field-studio generateDerivative (OffscreenCanvas+Worker, ingest.worker.ts:329) is liftable seed — extend to recursive pyramid loop → DZI level grid. wasm-vips (~13-20 MB) has no dzsave — do not use in main bundle. Decided v1.1 in CONTEXT.md §85. Unlocks biology/medical/cartography/photography contributors. Source: Prior Art/17-tile-generation.md; Prior Art/_GAP-ANSWERS.md.

name: Competitor lock-in taxonomy: three shapes
description: Three lock-in shapes dominate IIIF/annotation comparators: (1) server-data (Exhibit.so, Storiiies, ThingLink, Genially, Hypothes.is — data on vendor server, no export), (2) account/ecosystem (StoryMapJS/Google, Juncture/GitHub, Perusall/LMS), (3) subscription/credit (Transkribus credits, ThingLink paid export). Archie .archie.zip breaks all three simultaneously — contributor owns artifact from first save. Source: docs/research/contributor-appeal/01-competitor-contributor.md §2.1.

name: IIIF storytelling tools onboarding speed ranking
description: Time-to-first-value with no prior setup (2026 survey): Storiiies ~2 min no account, Hypothes.is ~2 min free account, Exhibit.so ~3 min no account, Padlet ~2 min free account, ThingLink/Genially ~5 min free account, StoryMapJS ~5 min Google account, Recogito ~5 min free account, Juncture ~10 min GitHub account, Scalar ~15 min free account, Canopy IIIF ~30 min GitHub+Node, Omeka S hours-days. Target: Archie Playground matches Exhibit.so at ~3 min. Source: docs/research/contributor-appeal/01-competitor-contributor.md §2.3.

name: No comparator combines: server-free async peer IIIF annotation + WADM + competing-interpretation + portable output
description: Biggest gap in 21-tool survey (2026): Every peer annotation tool (Recogito, Mirador MMU, Hypothes.is, Perusall) requires a shared server or platform account. Every IIIF storytelling tool (Exhibit.so, Storiiies, Juncture, Canopy) is read-only or single-author. No tool structures scholarly disagreement as first-class data (rival readings coexisting, not merged). Archie's combination of async-zip DAG collaboration + Readings model + IIIF P3 output + serverless publish has no comparator. Source: docs/research/contributor-appeal/01-competitor-contributor.md §4.

name: IIIF manifest URL paste as universal contributor on-ramp
description: Exhibit.so and Storiiies prove that pasting a IIIF manifest URL is the lowest-friction entry for IIIF-aware contributors — no re-upload, no account, instant access to 50,000+ institutional collections. Donor: cozy-iiif parseURL (Prior Art/14-import-interop.md:cozy-iiif/Cozy.ts:31,89) is PURE and ready to lift. Pattern: URL → fetch → classify (Manifest/Collection/Image) → P2→P3 normalize → scaffold Exhibit object. Source: docs/research/contributor-appeal/01-competitor-contributor.md §1.1, §1.2, §3.

name: EXIF to IIIF/WADM metadata ingest: unsolved gap, photographer audience
description: No comparator in 21-tool survey auto-populates IIIF metadata from EXIF. field-studio ingest.worker.ts:351 is a stub. Gap confirmed in Prior Art/_GAPS.md. Pattern: exifreader → navDate + provider metadata auto-fill. Broadens contributor base to photographers and field researchers who start from camera images, not blank forms. Client-side exifreader is serverless-safe. Donor: exifreader (tiff.js pattern). Priority P1 feature candidate. Source: docs/research/contributor-appeal/01-competitor-contributor.md §3.

name: Async-zip collaboration has no server-free IIIF precedent
description: Peer annotation collaboration in all 21 surveyed tools requires either a shared server (Recogito, Mirador MMU, papadam) or a platform account (Hypothes.is, Perusall, Zooniverse). Archie CONTEXT.md §182 decides async-zip DAG: share copy for review → import changes → silent DAG fast-forward + conflict-card resolution. This is genuinely novel. No prior-art code to lift — the merge algorithm and conflict-card UI are v1 inventions. Source: docs/research/contributor-appeal/01-competitor-contributor.md §2.4, §3.

name: local-image-import-blocker
description: In-browser server-free folder→static-IIIF manifest generation is zero-prior-art (Prior Art/_GAPS.md:1). Blocks 6 of 9 contributor personas (GLAM, community, journalist, genealogy, portfolio, map) from contributing their own content. biiif algorithm must be reimplemented against FSA/OPFS client-side. P0 for contributor-base broadening.

name: async-zip-collab-sweet-spot
description: Async-zip collaboration (export .archie.zip → email → import) is the right primitive for small peer groups (2–6 contributors) in GLAM peer review, scholarly collab, and journalist/editor pairs. Ceiling hits at classroom (30 students) and crowdsourcing (volunteer pools) — those are server-shaped and correctly deferred. v1 UI should communicate '2–6 contributors' as the designed scope to prevent expectation mismatch.

name: playground-template-diversity
description: A single manuscript-flavoured Playground template signals DH-only to non-specialist visitors and causes immediate segment dropout. Multiple templates (family photos, map exhibit, classroom exercise, photo essay) each as a pre-loaded .archie.zip are low-effort, high-leverage first impressions. Evidence: every platform comparison (Omeka, Scalar, Recogito) suffers from DH-first framing that excludes adjacent segments.

name: src-zip-url-as-non-github-publish-path
description: ?src=<zip-url> is the viable v1 publish path for non-technical contributors who cannot or will not create a GitHub account (community archivists, genealogists, portfolio creators). The mechanism exists (CONTEXT §224) but is currently 'cite-with-caveats' — needs UX elevation as a primary publish option with a clear durability warning. Not a fallback; for ~50% of target personas it is the only viable path.

name: IIIF-annotation-tooling-gap-scholarly
description: IIIF Annotations Community Group (iiif.io/community/groups/annotations/) formally states: 'the ecosystem of tooling does not yet exist to support scholarly annotation at scale; lack of consistent patterns is the structural gap.' Archie's Readings=AnnotationCollections + WADM-native storage is positioned to fill this gap for the exhibit-annotation use case. The gap is structural and well-documented, not a niche observation.

GLAM crowdsourcing projects (FromThePage, Zooniverse, NARA Citizen Archivist) consistently hit a wall when moving volunteer transcriptions into institutional CMSs: metadata field character limits, no bulk import, page-level vs item-level mismatch (Princeton Startwords 2021). Archie can serve as the 'final-mile exhibit layer' on top of existing crowdsourcing via CSV import of transcription data → WADM annotations. This is an unmet need with documented evidence.
resolution: CSV import path (Feature P3 in 02-user-service-design.md) would bridge this gap; deferred pending local-image-import P0.

title: non-technical publish path must foreground src-zip equally with GitHub Pages
rationale: GitHub Pages publish requires a GitHub account and CLI familiarity — hard ceiling for community, genealogy, portfolio, and journalist segments. ?src=<zip-url> already exists in CONTEXT §224 but is positioned as secondary/caveated. For broadened contributor base, the publish UI must surface both paths with equal prominence, with a clear durability explanation for ?src=. Decision: neither path is 'advanced'.

name: Tier-0 ① decomposition: the gap is the folder GESTURE, not the ingest pipeline
description: Scout 2026-06-09 (sub-cycle A of Archie-e1d6): Studio already ingests local files robustly — addObjectFromFile (App.svelte:512) does EXIF orientation baking, display-master downscale (§80 cap), OPFS persistence, AV branch; the editor rail's Choose-file input is multi-select. What 6/9 personas lack is folder→exhibit in ONE gesture from LibraryHome. Donor for the manifest half (gap-ledger 'Client-side folder → static-IIIF'): iiif-builder src/iiif-builder.ts:10 IIIFBuilder/:92 createManifest/:112 toPresentation3 (pure browser) — NOT needed for sub-cycle A because Archie's own publish pipeline already emits Presentation-3 from an exhibit. Sub-cycle A = webkitdirectory input on LibraryHome (Playwright setInputFiles supports directory upload; FSA showDirectoryPicker is Chromium-only) + pure helpers (root-name, hidden-file filter, natural sort by relative path) + loop over addObjectFromFile. Decomposition tracked under seed Archie-e1d6.

name: Tier-0 ② plan: lift cozy-iiif parseURL algorithm subset, not the dep
description: Scout 2026-06-09 (Archie-bc01): donor cozy-iiif Cozy.ts:31 parseURL (fetch->classify->normalize, P2->P3 via @iiif/parser convertPresentation2; Prior Art/14-import-interop.md:17-19). Archie needs only the {title, canvases->objects(source,label,w,h,mediaType)} subset; image source = body.service base preferred (matches existing object model — voynich objects ARE service bases) else body.id; P2 path = sequences->canvases->images->resource. Lift the algorithm, skip the @iiif/parser dep (repo is dep-frugal; studio bundle unbudgeted but simplicity rules). Collections deferred to a later sub-cycle (alert + seed note). e2e fixture: Archie's own published voynich manifest — publish<->import symmetry. addObject() probes dims via which can't load a bare service base — use appendObject with manifest-provided w/h instead.
